acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `--no-push` migration tests assert remote store refs are unchanged before/after.
- [ ] The tombstone/deleted-dep fixture asserts preserved note identity after migration.
- [ ] Tests lock preservation of existing `root_slug` and `last_write_stamp` when migration rewrites an already-populated meta.
- [ ] Fixture docs reflect actual note/backfill behavior and scope.
description:
**Problem**
The adversarial review found that the realistic migration suite still leaves several contract gaps. The `--no-push` cases do not assert that origin stayed untouched, the tombstone/deleted-dep fixture contains embedded notes whose preservation is not asserted, and the fixture corpus does not lock the metadata-preservation contract for existing `root_slug` / `last_write_stamp` values.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/README.md, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/*, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Strengthen the migration suite so it proves `--no-push` never mutates origin, asserts note identity for the tombstone fixture as well as the rich fixtures, and reads back migrated `meta.json` to lock preservation of existing metadata fields. Update the corpus README where the current fixture description omits note backfill behavior or overstates what the fixture proves.
title: Migration follow-up: close remaining coverage gaps around no-push isolation, tombstone notes, and metadata preservation
